alternatives.name: Cold-drawn near-net steel profiles (ZOLLERN)
alternatives.tradeoffs: IT8 tolerance (+-0.02 mm); eliminates 40-60% secondary machining for receivers, slide rails, bolt carriers. Weakness: 6-12 week tooling lead; 100 kg min batch; cannot IH short parts in-house (C9); ITAR/EAR compliance required (C13). ~25% share.

alternatives.name: Drop / hammer forged blanks
alternatives.tradeoffs: Superior grain structure for high-stress military receivers; dominant for M16 upper/lower forgings; barrel forging at H&K/Walther produces rifling simultaneously. Weakness: longer die lead + forging trial; flash removal adds machining step; +-0.5-1.0 mm tolerance. ~45% share.

alternatives.name: CNC billet machining from bar stock
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero tooling lead; single-piece flexibility; +-0.01 mm machined tolerance. 60-70% material waste; highest machining cycle time; uneconomic at volume; scores 10/10 on all flexibility needs. ~25% share.

alternatives.name: Investment casting (trigger groups, small parts)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Complex 3D geometries; +-0.3-0.5 mm accuracy; not suited for constant-cross-section profiles; limited to trigger groups and small structural components (PCC, Hitchiner). ~5% share.

alternatives.name: No measurement / continue billet machining (Status Quo)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero switching cost; zero new supplier qualification; retains all machining waste; zero acquisition burden. Dominant competitor because there is no P1 friction.
